case_title: CAPITAL ONE, N.A. VS. LESTER J ROJAS

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE, N.A.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL AND ENTER JUDGMENT UNDER STIPULATION IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) NO PROPOSED ORDER NOR PROPOSED JUDGMENT RECEIVED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE AND FOLLOW THE COURT'S ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING, A PROPOSED JUDGMENT AND MUST DROP A HARD COURTESY COPY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Enter Judgment Under Stipulation
Off Calendar Jun-01-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Capital One, N.A.'s Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Enter Judgment Under Stipulation is GRANTED.

The request to vacate dismissal is granted. The court orders any dismissal entered in this action vacated.

Good cause appearing and consistent with the parties' agreement and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, judgment shall be entered as follows: Plaintiff Capital One, N.A. shall recover from Defendant Lester J. Rojas $1,800 in principal and $578.50 in costs, for a total judgment of $2,378.50. This is the judgment authorized by the settlement agreement and related events.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above text verbatim.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court, ideally on the appropriate Judicial Council form.

No proposed order nor proposed judgment received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling, a proposed judgment and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
